Research_Summary: Standardized framework for microbiome ML; benchmarks CRC datasets to show impact of model choice.
ML_Approach_Utility: Emphasizes data splitting and cross-validation to prevent leakage and overfitting.
Algorithms_Applied: RF, SVM, L2-Logistic Regression, XGBoost, Decision Trees
Hardware_Notes: Scales via Snakemake; excellent for multi-core HPC.

Research_Summary: Meta-analysis of WGS CRC studies across global cohorts to find consistent microbial signatures.
ML_Approach_Utility: LASSO models for high-dimensionality/sparsity with rigorous cross-study validation.
Hardware_Notes: Runs in R (SIAMCAT); parallelizable across standard nodes.

Research_Summary: Created ML Repo: 33 curated tasks from 15 microbiome datasets for standardized benchmarking.
ML_Approach_Utility: Standardized data inputs specifically to test, train, and compare ML algorithms.
Hardware_Notes: Lightweight; processed data runs fast on standard CPUs.

Research_Summary: Multi-class model differentiating 9 phenotypes using fecal metagenomics from 2,300+ individuals.
ML_Approach_Utility: Multi-class classification to prevent misdiagnosis in patients with overlapping signatures.
Hardware_Notes: Scales well with R doParallel; multi-core friendly.

Research_Summary: Systematic assessment (MetAML) of ML for WGS data across T2D, CRC, and IBD.
ML_Approach_Utility: Compared RF vs SVM; highlighted RF performance with species-level abundance.
Hardware_Notes: Python scikit-learn; uses n_jobs=-1 for CPU scaling.

Research_Summary: Compiled MicrobiomeHD database to differentiate disease-specific vs. general taxonomic shifts.
ML_Approach_Utility: Random Forest with out-of-bag error estimation to validate disease-specific predictors.
